insertions.translation_zh: AIA founder Mr. Starr Mr. Cornelius Vander Starr established a company in Shanghai as an insurance agency, laying the Group's roots in Asia.
insertions.target_text: AIA founder Mr. Starr Mr. Cornelius Vander Starr established a company in Shanghai as an insurance agency, laying the Group's roots in Asia.

insertions.translation_zh: Mr. Starr founded in Shanghai his first life insurance business, Asia Life Insurance Company。
insertions.target_text: Mr. Starr founded in Shanghai his first life insurance business, Asia Life Insurance Company。

insertions.translation_zh: Mr. Starr founded in Shanghai a company called International Assurance Company, Limited (INTASCO).
insertions.target_text: Mr. Starr founded in Shanghai a company called International Assurance Company, Limited (INTASCO).

insertions.translation_zh: INTASCO established branches in Hong Kong and Singapore.
insertions.target_text: INTASCO established branches in Hong Kong and Singapore.

insertions.translation_zh: Founded The in the Philippines Philippine American Life and General Insurance Company (Philam Life)。
insertions.target_text: Founded The in the Philippines Philippine American Life and General Insurance Company (Philam Life)。

insertions.translation_zh: INTASCO moved its headquarters to Hong Kong.
insertions.target_text: INTASCO moved its headquarters to Hong Kong.

insertions.translation_zh: INTASCO was renamed American International Assurance Company, Limited。
insertions.target_text: INTASCO was renamed American International Assurance Company, Limited。

insertions.translation_zh: Through our Shanghai branch we resumed business in China, becoming the first licensed foreign life insurer in China.
insertions.target_text: Through our Shanghai branch we resumed business in China, becoming the first licensed foreign life insurer in China.

insertions.translation_zh: We celebrated our return to our original headquarters building on the Bund in Shanghai.
insertions.target_text: We celebrated our return to our original headquarters building on the Bund in Shanghai.

insertions.translation_zh: We completed the restructuring caused by AIG's financial crisis in 2008, which laid the foundation for the Company's listing plan laying the foundation.
insertions.target_text: We completed the restructuring caused by AIG's financial crisis in 2008, which laid the foundation for the Company's listing plan laying the foundation.

insertions.translation_zh: AIA Group Limited successfully listed on the Main Board of The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited, then the world's third largest initial public offering.
insertions.target_text: AIA Group Limited successfully listed on the Main Board of The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited, then the world's third largest initial public offering.

insertions.translation_zh: AIA Group Limited became a constituent of the Hang Seng Index.
insertions.target_text: AIA Group Limited became a constituent of the Hang Seng Index.

insertions.translation_zh: We established a sponsored Level 1 American Depositary Receipt programme.
insertions.target_text: We established a sponsored Level 1 American Depositary Receipt programme.

insertions.translation_zh: AIA completed the full integration with ING's Malaysian operations integration.
insertions.target_text: AIA completed the full integration with ING's Malaysian operations integration.

insertions.translation_zh: Through the acquisition of Aviva NDB Insurance, we began operations in Sri Lanka.
insertions.target_text: Through the acquisition of Aviva NDB Insurance, we began operations in Sri Lanka.

insertions.translation_zh: AIA and Citibank entered into a significant exclusive long-term bancassurance partnership covering 11 markets across Asia-Pacific.
insertions.target_text: AIA and Citibank entered into a significant exclusive long-term bancassurance partnership covering 11 markets across Asia-Pacific.

insertions.translation_zh: AIA became Tottenham Hotspur Football Club's official shirt partner, promoting sport as an important part of healthy living.
insertions.target_text: AIA became Tottenham Hotspur Football Club's official shirt partner, promoting sport as an important part of healthy living.

insertions.translation_zh: AIA's Million Dollar Round Table membership became the largest in the world.
insertions.target_text: AIA's Million Dollar Round Table membership became the largest in the world.

insertions.translation_zh: Opened AIA in Bangkok Leadership Centre。
insertions.target_text: Opened AIA in Bangkok Leadership Centre。

insertions.translation_zh: AIA increased its stake in the Indian joint venture Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited, from 26% to 49%.
insertions.target_text: AIA increased its stake in the Indian joint venture Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited, from 26% to 49%.

insertions.translation_zh: AIA presents the Hong Kong Observation Wheel and AIA Vitality Park.
insertions.target_text: AIA presents the Hong Kong Observation Wheel and AIA Vitality Park.

insertions.translation_zh: AIA launched a new brand promise: Healthier, Longer, Better Lives.
insertions.target_text: AIA launched a new brand promise: Healthier, Longer, Better Lives.

insertions.translation_zh: Since 1919 committed to advancing Asia's economic and social development
insertions.target_text: Since 1919 committed to advancing Asia's economic and social development

insertions.translation_zh: Notes: (1) The financial information from 2017 to 2019 is presented on a fiscal year ended 31 December basis . The financial information from 2015 to 2016 is presented on a fiscal year ended 30 November basis. For clarity, growth rates are presented on a fiscal year ended 31 December basis.
insertions.target_text: Notes: (1) The financial information from 2017 to 2019 is presented on a fiscal year ended 31 December basis . The financial information from 2015 to 2016 is presented on a fiscal year ended 30 November basis. For clarity, growth rates are presented on a fiscal year ended 31 December basis.

insertions.translation_zh: (2) Value of new business is the present value, measured at the point of sale, of expected future after-tax statutory profits from new business sold during the period less the cost of holding capital in excess of statutory reserves required to support the new business.
insertions.target_text: (2) Value of new business is the present value, measured at the point of sale, of expected future after-tax statutory profits from new business sold during the period less the cost of holding capital in excess of statutory reserves required to support the new business.

insertions.translation_zh: (3) Annualised new premiums are a measure of new business activity, calculated as 100% of annualised 100% of first-year premiums plus 10% of single premiums.
insertions.target_text: (3) Annualised new premiums are a measure of new business activity, calculated as 100% of annualised 100% of first-year premiums plus 10% of single premiums.

insertions.translation_zh: (4) Operating profit after tax shown is net of non-controlling interests.
insertions.target_text: (4) Operating profit after tax shown is net of non-controlling interests.

insertions.translation_zh: (5) Total weighted premium income consists of 100% of renewal premiums before reinsurance ceded, first-year premiums at 100%, plus 10% of single premiums.
insertions.target_text: (5) Total weighted premium income consists of 100% of renewal premiums before reinsurance ceded, first-year premiums at 100%, plus 10% of single premiums.

insertions.translation_zh: (6) Embedded value is the estimated economic value of the life insurance business determined by actuarial methods based on a set of specified assumptions about future experience but excluding the economic value of any future new business. Embedded value equity is the sum of embedded value, goodwill and other intangible assets.
insertions.target_text: (6) Embedded value is the estimated economic value of the life insurance business determined by actuarial methods based on a set of specified assumptions about future experience but excluding the economic value of any future new business. Embedded value equity is the sum of embedded value, goodwill and other intangible assets.

insertions.translation_zh: (7) Segmental value of new business includes pension business, is shown on a local statutory basis, before unallocated Group Office expenses and before deducting non-controlling interests.
insertions.target_text: (7) Segmental value of new business includes pension business, is shown on a local statutory basis, before unallocated Group Office expenses and before deducting non-controlling interests.

insertions.translation_zh: (8) In 2019, annualised new premiums and value of new business for Other Markets include our India joint venture Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited (Tata AIA Life) at 49% of results to reflect our shareholding. The annualised new premiums reported for 2018 and value of new business were not restated and do not include any contribution from Tata AIA Life. The Group's overall value of new business reported for 2019 excludes new business value attributable to non-controlling interests. Value of new business for 2018 was not restated; as previously disclosed in the 2018 Annual Report, that figure was reported before deducting non-controlling interests. Tata AIA Life's IFRS results are accounted for using the equity method. The Group's consolidated financial statements use Tata AIA Life financial information on a one-quarter lag basis. For clarity, total weighted premium income excludes any contribution from Tata AIA Life.
insertions.target_text: (8) In 2019, annualised new premiums and value of new business for Other Markets include our India joint venture Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited (Tata AIA Life) at 49% of results to reflect our shareholding. The annualised new premiums reported for 2018 and value of new business were not restated and do not include any contribution from Tata AIA Life. The Group's overall value of new business reported for 2019 excludes new business value attributable to non-controlling interests. Value of new business for 2018 was not restated; as previously disclosed in the 2018 Annual Report, that figure was reported before deducting non-controlling interests. Tata AIA Life's IFRS results are accounted for using the equity method. The Group's consolidated financial statements use Tata AIA Life financial information on a one-quarter lag basis. For clarity, total weighted premium income excludes any contribution from Tata AIA Life.

insertions.translation_zh: Notes: (1) In 2019, annualised new premiums and value of new business for Other Markets include 49% of results from Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited (Tata AIA Life), reflecting our shareholding. Its contributions to annualised new premiums and value of new business were US$182 million and US$38 million, respectively. The Group's overall value of new business reported for 2019 has deducted non-controlling interests' share of value of new business of US$32 million. For 2018, annualised new premiums and value of new business were not restated and exclude any contribution from Tata AIA Life, and did not deduct amounts attributable to non-controlling interests. As disclosed in the 2018 Annual Report, value of new business attributable to non-controlling interests in 2018 was US$27 million.
insertions.target_text: Notes: (1) In 2019, annualised new premiums and value of new business for Other Markets include 49% of results from Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited (Tata AIA Life), reflecting our shareholding. Its contributions to annualised new premiums and value of new business were US$182 million and US$38 million, respectively. The Group's overall value of new business reported for 2019 has deducted non-controlling interests' share of value of new business of US$32 million. For 2018, annualised new premiums and value of new business were not restated and exclude any contribution from Tata AIA Life, and did not deduct amounts attributable to non-controlling interests. As disclosed in the 2018 Annual Report, value of new business attributable to non-controlling interests in 2018 was US$27 million.

insertions.translation_zh:
Value of new business increased by 6% to US$4,154 million in 2019, driven by very strong growth in Mainland China and Other Markets, largely offset by Hong Kong's 5% decline due to the sharp fall in value of new business from Mainland Chinese visitor customers amid social unrest in the second half of 2019. offset by a 5% decline caused by a sharp fall in value of new business from Mainland Chinese visitor customers amid social unrest in the second half of 2019.

Annualised new premiums increased by 2% to US$6,585 million, and the value of new business margin rose by 3.0 percentage points to 62.9%. The margin calculated on present value of new business premiums increased from 10% in 2018 to 11%. Despite lower interest rates in the second half of 2019, the 2019 value of new business margin and the margin on present value of new business premiums both improved, supported by higher profitability of long-term savings and protection products in Hong Kong and the positive effect of increased commission tax credits from changes in Mainland China tax rules positive impact from increased commission tax credits.

Agency distribution accounted for 74% of the Group's overall value of new business. We continued to execute the Premier Agency strategy, growing its value of new business by 11% to US$3,243 million while the number of active agents increased by 5%. Partnership distribution value of new business decreased by 1% to US$1,142 million. Strong growth in bancassurance was offset by a sharp decline in new business from Hong Kong's retail IFA channel in the second half of 2019, reflecting lower visitor arrivals and intensified competition.
insertions.target_text:
Value of new business increased by 6% to US$4,154 million in 2019, driven by very strong growth in Mainland China and Other Markets, largely offset by Hong Kong's 5% decline due to the sharp fall in value of new business from Mainland Chinese visitor customers amid social unrest in the second half of 2019. offset by a 5% decline caused by a sharp fall in value of new business from Mainland Chinese visitor customers amid social unrest in the second half of 2019.

Annualised new premiums increased by 2% to US$6,585 million, and the value of new business margin rose by 3.0 percentage points to 62.9%. The margin calculated on present value of new business premiums increased from 10% in 2018 to 11%. Despite lower interest rates in the second half of 2019, the 2019 value of new business margin and the margin on present value of new business premiums both improved, supported by higher profitability of long-term savings and protection products in Hong Kong and the positive effect of increased commission tax credits from changes in Mainland China tax rules positive impact from increased commission tax credits.

Agency distribution accounted for 74% of the Group's overall value of new business. We continued to execute the Premier Agency strategy, growing its value of new business by 11% to US$3,243 million while the number of active agents increased by 5%. Partnership distribution value of new business decreased by 1% to US$1,142 million. Strong growth in bancassurance was offset by a sharp decline in new business from Hong Kong's retail IFA channel in the second half of 2019, reflecting lower visitor arrivals and intensified competition.

insertions.translation_zh:
Excluding Hong Kong, the Group's overall value of new business grew strongly by 16% compared with 2018. Also excluding Hong Kong, the Group's agency distribution new business value increased by 16%, while partnership distribution value of new business increased by 19%.

Hong Kong recorded a 5% decrease in value of new business in 2019. Double-digit growth in value of new business from the domestic customer segment was offset by a sharp decline in value of new business from Mainland Chinese visitors which was broadly consistent with the decline in Mainland Chinese visitors to Hong Kong in the second half of 2019 reported by the Hong Kong Tourism Board. New business value of new business margin increased by 4.1 percentage points to 66.1%, mainly driven by improved profitability of our long-term savings and protection products.

Mainland China delivered very strong value of new business growth of 27% to US$1,167 million. The Premier Agency strategy supported double-digit growth in active agents and further improved productivity. The value of new business margin increased by 3.1 percentage points to 93.5%, due to the positive impact of higher commission tax credits from changes in Mainland China tax rules, partially offset by enhanced policyholder benefits in our protection products.

Thailand continued to benefit from our Financial Adviser programme and good progress in the exclusive partnership with Bangkok Bank Public Company Limited (Bangkok Bank) and recorded overall value of new business growth of 6% to US$494 million in 2019. The value of new business margin decreased to 67.7%, mainly because of the increased proportion of bancassurance business.

Singapore's value of new business in 2019 remained stable at US$352 million, as growth in regular premium sales was offset by lower single premium sales through the partnership distribution channel.

Malaysia achieved 7% value of new business growth to US$258 million in 2019, supported by increased sales through both agency and branch-based bancassurance channels supporting the growth.

Other Markets recorded very strong value of new business growth of 27% to US$535 million, driven by Australia, the Philippines and Vietnam.

The Group's reported value of new business for 2019 was reduced by US$273 million, representing consolidated reserving and capital requirements in excess of local statutory requirements, unallocated present value of Group Office expenses and value of new business attributable to non-controlling interests.

In 2019, annualised new premiums and value of new business for Other Markets include 49% of results from Tata AIA Life to reflect our shareholding. Its contribution to annualised new premiums and value of new business were US$182 million and US$38 million, respectively. The Group's overall value of new business reported for 2019 has deducted value of new business attributable to non-controlling interests of US$32 million. For 2018, annualised new premiums and value of new business were not restated and exclude any contribution from Tata AIA Life and did not deduct amounts attributable to non-controlling interests. As disclosed in the 2018 Annual Report, 2018 value of new business attributable to non-controlling interests was US$27 million.
insertions.target_text:
Excluding Hong Kong, the Group's overall value of new business grew strongly by 16% compared with 2018. Also excluding Hong Kong, the Group's agency distribution new business value increased by 16%, while partnership distribution value of new business increased by 19%.

Hong Kong recorded a 5% decrease in value of new business in 2019. Double-digit growth in value of new business from the domestic customer segment was offset by a sharp decline in value of new business from Mainland Chinese visitors which was broadly consistent with the decline in Mainland Chinese visitors to Hong Kong in the second half of 2019 reported by the Hong Kong Tourism Board. New business value of new business margin increased by 4.1 percentage points to 66.1%, mainly driven by improved profitability of our long-term savings and protection products.

Mainland China delivered very strong value of new business growth of 27% to US$1,167 million. The Premier Agency strategy supported double-digit growth in active agents and further improved productivity. The value of new business margin increased by 3.1 percentage points to 93.5%, due to the positive impact of higher commission tax credits from changes in Mainland China tax rules, partially offset by enhanced policyholder benefits in our protection products.

Thailand continued to benefit from our Financial Adviser programme and good progress in the exclusive partnership with Bangkok Bank Public Company Limited (Bangkok Bank) and recorded overall value of new business growth of 6% to US$494 million in 2019. The value of new business margin decreased to 67.7%, mainly because of the increased proportion of bancassurance business.

Singapore's value of new business in 2019 remained stable at US$352 million, as growth in regular premium sales was offset by lower single premium sales through the partnership distribution channel.

Malaysia achieved 7% value of new business growth to US$258 million in 2019, supported by increased sales through both agency and branch-based bancassurance channels supporting the growth.

Other Markets recorded very strong value of new business growth of 27% to US$535 million, driven by Australia, the Philippines and Vietnam.

The Group's reported value of new business for 2019 was reduced by US$273 million, representing consolidated reserving and capital requirements in excess of local statutory requirements, unallocated present value of Group Office expenses and value of new business attributable to non-controlling interests.

In 2019, annualised new premiums and value of new business for Other Markets include 49% of results from Tata AIA Life to reflect our shareholding. Its contribution to annualised new premiums and value of new business were US$182 million and US$38 million, respectively. The Group's overall value of new business reported for 2019 has deducted value of new business attributable to non-controlling interests of US$32 million. For 2018, annualised new premiums and value of new business were not restated and exclude any contribution from Tata AIA Life and did not deduct amounts attributable to non-controlling interests. As disclosed in the 2018 Annual Report, 2018 value of new business attributable to non-controlling interests was US$27 million.
